MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Natalie Ann Garcia of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Vincent Seddo

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Bruce Fraser are sustained. The Court will point out that it did not grant summary judgment based on defendant's arguments related to substantial factor causation and duty. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
